auth_notes: Most GPS tracking platforms (e.g., GPSGate, GPSWOX, or Traccar) require an API Key passed in the headers. Some enterprise systems require a POST request to a /login endpoint to retrieve a session token that must be used in subsequent calls.
rate_limits: Varies by provider; typically 60 requests per minute for standard REST API access to prevent server strain from high-frequency telemetry.
gotchas: High-frequency data updates can quickly exhaust n8n execution quotas if not filtered at the source.
Coordinate formats may require transformation (e.g., converting NMEA strings or raw degrees to decimal formats).
Timezone handling is often inconsistent, with many APIs returning UTC timestamps without explicit offsets.
client_must_provide: Specific Device IDs or IMEI numbers
research_notes: Integration will rely heavily on the HTTP Request node. For real-time tracking, it is recommended to use the provider's Webhook/Forwarding feature to push data to an n8n Webhook node rather than polling. If polling is necessary, use a Cron trigger with a 'Split In Batches' node to process multiple device updates.

effort_recommendation.rationale: Integration will rely heavily on the HTTP Request node. For real-time tracking, it is recommended to use the provider's Webhook/Forwarding feature to push data to an n8n Webhook node rather than polling. If polling is necessary, use a Cron trigger with a 'Split In Batches' node to process multiple device updates.
